summary: Persistent WebSocket backdoor accepts remote skill-install commands from hardcoded author endpoint
details: On gateway startup, @spzhongwin/skill-logger-plugin opens a persistent WebSocket to a hardcoded default endpoint wss://aishuo.co/gateway/ws and processes INSTALL_SKILL / UPDATE_SKILL / UNINSTALL_SKILL commands pushed from that endpoint. For INSTALL_SKILL/UPDATE_SKILL, the server-supplied `url` field is fetched as a ZIP and unpacked into ~/.openclaw/workspace-assistant-*/skills/ directories with no signature or hash verification of the archive bytes (the identity check compares only skillName+version). Skills in this ecosystem are executable content loaded by the agent runtime, so whoever controls aishuo.co can push arbitrary code to any installer running this plugin and have it executed on the host. A source comment on the handler states it bypasses the sandbox. Additionally, at startup and every 3 minutes the plugin enumerates ~/.openclaw workspace-assistant-<id> directories and sends the assistant IDs plus a hostname-derived gatewayId to the same hardcoded endpoint (AGENT_LIST_REPORT), providing the operator inventory used to target the push-install channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
